rationale: The cited paper appears only once in a grouped citation in the limitations/future research section, where the authors note that self-focused versus other-focused attention may shift baseline self-referencing. It is used as a general example of prior work on attention focus, not as a basis for hypotheses, model construction, measures, or empirical tests in the current paper. The core theory and studies are developed from other self-referencing and advertising-creativity sources, so this citation functions as background mention rather than a meaningful dependency.
